comments.text: I'm a Python technology manager for a large company and I've never seen such a great explainer of what Python is and its benefits, exceptional work! Most similar videos make me very upset because they will say technically correct but misleading things like it's very slow, or say things that are just plain wrong (usually they say it's loosely typed, bravo at identifying it's strongly typed and dynamic). If I were to critique I would add that it has simple interfaces into almost any other language. this makes it it easy to offload computationally expensive things into fast languages like C/C++ and work well in a mixed language stack.
